description_long: This is a vintage paddle steamer boat with a wooden hull and two large side-mounted paddle wheels, each supported by a visible framework of struts and gears. The deck features a central raised section, likely for the engine or control area, and is surrounded by open space for passengers or cargo. The paddle wheels are constructed with multiple spokes and paddles, designed to propel the boat through water by rotating. The hull is elongated and tapers to a point at the bow, with a flat stern, typical of riverboats used in the 19th century. The structure appears robust, with metal reinforcements and mechanical linkages visible around the paddle wheels. The overall color palette is dominated by weathered wood tones and dark metallic elements, giving it an industrial, historical appearance.
description: A vintage paddle steamer boat with a wooden hull and large side-mounted paddle wheels, featuring visible gears and struts. The boat has a weathered, industrial look with a central raised deck section.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed bow, central deck, and symmetrical paddle wheels on either side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the hull and the detailed structure of the paddle wheel and gears.
description_view_2: Top-down view revealing the open deck layout, central raised section, and the arrangement of the paddle wheels.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the other paddle wheel and the flat stern of the boat.
